metadata.summary: Layered RSpec testing for Rails: model specs, service specs, request specs, system specs. BDD structure with factory_bot, shoulda-matchers, SimpleCov.

content.title: RSpec Testing for Rails Applications
content.summary: Build comprehensive Rails test suites using layered RSpec strategies: model specs test validations and scopes, service specs test business logic, request specs test HTTP contracts, and system specs test browser behavior. Use factory_bot for test data, shoulda-matchers for one-liners, and SimpleCov branch tracking to find uncovered paths. BDD structure (describe/context/it) enforces one behavior per test block and produces diagnostic failure messages.

why.p: RSpec's BDD structure enforces a single behavior per it block, making failure messages diagnostic without reading the test body. build_stubbed eliminates DB writes for model unit tests (10-100x faster). Layer isolation — model specs test validations, service specs test business logic, request specs test HTTP contracts — means agent edits to one layer do not invalidate tests for another. SimpleCov branch tracking finds uncovered conditional paths that line coverage misses.

list.item: Rails app with layered behavior: models with validations, PORO services, REST endpoints.
TDD or red/green/refactor cycles with LLM agents — RSpec DSL maps to agent prompts well.
Codebases enforcing coverage gates (simplecov ≥80% for services).
Multi-developer teams using shared examples and shared contexts to reduce duplication.
Refactor-heavy phases where fast model + service specs are the safety net.
Greenfield Hanami/Roda/Sinatra apps — rails_helper and Rails matchers do not apply.
Pure CLI gems — spec_helper only; rails_helper is overkill.
Codebases standardized on Minitest — mixing creates two test infrastructures.
Performance benchmarks — use benchmark/ips, not RSpec.
Visual regression — use Percy/Chromatic via Capybara, but the value is in the visual-diff service, not RSpec itself.

section.name: rspec-rules
section.p: Core rules for organizing and writing RSpec tests in Rails.

rule.statement: One describe per public method, one context per branch, one it per assertion.
rule.rationale: Keeps failure messages diagnostic: "Users::CreateService#call with invalid params does not create a user" pinpoints the failing behavior without reading the test body.

rule.statement: Name subject explicitly: subject(:service) { described_class.new(params: params) }.
rule.rationale: Unnamed subject { described_class.new } is unreadable when nested three contexts deep.

rule.statement: Use instance_double not double for service/repository fakes.
rule.rationale: instance_double verifies the method actually exists on the class; double lets agents stub nonexistent methods silently.

rule.statement: Prefer build_stubbed over create whenever no DB write is required.
rule.rationale: build_stubbed skips the database entirely; model unit tests are 10-100x faster with no schema dependency.

rule.statement: Avoid let! unless setup must run regardless of reference; prefer lazy let.
rule.rationale: let! triggers a DB write unconditionally; lazy let only writes when the variable is referenced, keeping tests that do not need the record fast.

rule.statement: Mirror the source-tree structure in spec/: spec/services/users/create_service_spec.rb for app/services/users/create_service.rb.
rule.rationale: SimpleCov groups coverage by file path; mismatched paths break per-file gap reporting.

rule.statement: Use :transaction strategy for all non-JS specs; use :truncation only for Capybara JS specs.
rule.rationale: Transaction rollback is the fastest cleanup; Capybara JS specs spawn a browser thread that cannot share the DB transaction, so truncation is required.

rule.statement: Standardize the strategy per spec type via RSpec.configure, never per individual spec.
rule.rationale: Per-spec overrides create inconsistency; agents copying the nearest before block apply the wrong strategy and introduce intermittent failures.

rule.statement: Pin time via ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers travel_to with the block form; always travel_back after.
rule.rationale: Without the block form, time stays frozen for subsequent specs. travel_to { ... } guarantees cleanup automatically.

rule.statement: Stub external HTTP with WebMock; record cassettes with VCR against staging; review cassette diffs on every API update.
rule.rationale: VCR replays old cassettes silently even when the API contract changed; periodic VCR_RECORD_MODE=all reruns expose drift.

section.name: rspec-examples
section.p: Worked examples of model, service, and request specs following best practices.

example.title: Model Spec with Shoulda-Matchers
example.note: build(:user) not create(:user) for the full_name test — no DB write needed.

example.title: Service Spec with AAA Structure
example.note: named subject, explicit contexts, one assertion per it block.

example.title: Request Spec
example.note: type: :request uses Rack::Test — no browser; fast HTTP-layer tests.

section.name: database-and-time-strategies
section.p: Critical strategies for avoiding test flakes and performance cliffs.

rule.statement: Use :transaction strategy for all non-JS specs; use :truncation only for Capybara JS specs.
rule.rationale: Transaction rollback is the fastest cleanup. Capybara JS specs spawn a browser thread that cannot share the DB transaction.

rule.statement: Standardize the strategy per spec type via RSpec.configure, never per individual spec.
rule.rationale: Per-spec overrides create inconsistency. Agents copying the nearest before block apply the wrong strategy and introduce intermittent failures.

rule.statement: Pin time via ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers travel_to with the block form; always travel_back after.
rule.rationale: Without the block form, time stays frozen for subsequent specs. travel_to { ... } guarantees cleanup automatically.

rule.statement: Stub external HTTP with WebMock; record cassettes with VCR against staging; review cassette diffs on every API update.
rule.rationale: VCR replays old cassettes silently even when the API contract changed. Periodic VCR_RECORD_MODE=all reruns expose drift.

section.name: rspec-antipatterns
section.p: Common failure modes in LLM-generated RSpec specs: stubbing the SUT, let shadowing, before(:all) mistakes, database cleaner mismatches, and time-dependent bugs.

antipattern.description: Stubbing the method under test: agent stubs the very service method being spec'd — spec always passes regardless of the implementation.
antipattern.reason: Stubbing the SUT detaches the test from the real code path. Force prompts to explicitly name what is mocked vs real before generating the spec.

antipattern.description: let shadowing: inner context redefines let(:user) with different attributes; outer spec examples silently use the wrong user.
antipattern.reason: RSpec resolves let from the nearest enclosing scope, not the outermost. Lint via rubocop-rspec ContextLeakCheck to catch redefined lets.

antipattern.description: before(:all) for DB setup: seed records created before the suite but within a transactional spec — transaction rolls back the seed, second run finds nothing.
antipattern.reason: before(:all) runs outside the per-example transaction. Use before(:each) or let! for records that must exist per example. Use shared_context for reusable setup.

antipattern.description: build_stubbed with AR associations queried via count: user.posts.count issues a SQL query; build_stubbed does not persist to DB, so count returns 0 or errors.
antipattern.reason: build_stubbed stubs AR persistence methods but not all query interfaces. For association assertions, allow(user).to receive(:posts).and_return([post]) or use create.

antipattern.description: travel_to without the block form: time freezes and never resets; subsequent specs run with stale frozen time.
antipattern.reason: Without travel_back or the block form, ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers leaves time pinned globally for the rest of the suite.

antipattern.description: Factory cascade bloat: create(:order) triggers user → profile → avatar creation; one spec inserts 5 records, slowing the suite by an order of magnitude.
antipattern.reason: Factory traits and associations compose automatically; agents copy the nearest factory call. Use build_stubbed or define traits without auto-associations.

antipattern.description: Shoulda-matchers on uncalled validators: validate_uniqueness_of(:email) passes even without a DB unique index, giving false confidence.
antipattern.reason: Shoulda-matchers check model validations in memory; they do not probe the DB constraint. Pair with a request spec that hits the actual constraint.

antipattern.description: VCR cassette drift: cassettes recorded against staging, replayed in CI, miss new fields; specs pass while integration is broken.
antipattern.reason: Cassettes capture a snapshot of the response. When API contracts change, cassettes are stale but tests pass. Periodically re-record and review diffs.

antipattern.description: Shared contexts hide behavior. A shared_context "as admin" mutates state in 8 ways; agent reading the spec sees only 1 line.
antipattern.reason: Hidden setup makes debugging failures harder. Use shared examples for cross-cutting behavior assertions, shared contexts for reusable setup.

antipattern.description: should-style matchers mixed with expect-style: codebases end up bilingual. RSpec disabled should for new specs but legacy lingers.
antipattern.reason: Inconsistent style makes it harder for agents to generate matching code. Enforce expect-style only.

antipattern.description: Slow suite drift: naïve let! + before(:each) patterns rebuild the world per spec; suite hits 10+ min after a few hundred specs.
antipattern.reason: Eager setup with let! creates DB writes per spec. Lazy let and build_stubbed keep suites under 1 min.

antipattern.description: Mock-heavy specs that pass when refactored wrong. Agents stub everything; tests assert on stubs, not behavior. Mutation testing exposes.
antipattern.reason: Stubbing the SUT detaches tests from real code paths. Mock at boundaries, not internals. Verify real behavior not stubbed behavior.

rule.statement: Prefer build_stubbed over create whenever possible. No DB write; specs are 10-100x faster for model unit tests.
rule.rationale: Schema dependencies and DB latency are the primary scaling bottleneck in test suites.

rule.statement: One describe per public method, context per branch, it per assertion. Keeps failure messages diagnostic.
rule.rationale: Diagnostic output reduces debugging friction.

rule.statement: Avoid let! unless setup must run regardless of usage. Lazy let is the default; eager triggers DB writes you do not need.
rule.rationale: Unnecessary DB writes slow the suite without improving test isolation.

rule.statement: subject named (subject(:service)); unnamed subject makes specs unreadable when nested.
rule.rationale: Readability improves debugging and agent spec generation.

rule.statement: Use instance_double not double. Verifies the method actually exists on the SUT.
rule.rationale: Agents can stub nonexistent methods with double, creating false-positive tests.

rule.statement: Mock at the boundary, not internals. Mock the HTTP gateway, not the service that calls the gateway. Otherwise refactor breaks tests.
rule.rationale: Tests should verify behavior contracts, not implementation details.

rule.statement: Use shared examples for cross-cutting behavior (auth, pagination); use shared contexts for setup blocks. Do not mix.
rule.rationale: Semantic separation keeps intent clear and reduces copy-paste.

rule.statement: System specs use Cuprite headless Chrome. Selenium is historical default — slower and flakier.
rule.rationale: Cuprite uses Chrome DevTools Protocol directly, eliminating WebDriver overhead.

rule.statement: Run bundle exec rspec --bisect after any flake. Order-dependence is debt that compounds.
rule.rationale: Test ordering bugs are exponentially harder to debug as the suite grows.

rule.statement: Mutation testing on critical services (auth, billing). Coverage alone hides assertion-free specs.
rule.rationale: Coverage metrics count lines executed, not assertions verified. Mutation testing forces true test quality.

rule.statement: Tag specs with metadata (:slow, :integration, :flaky_known); CI selects subsets per stage.
rule.rationale: Allows fast feedback loops (run fast specs) without sacrificing comprehensive CI.
